name: Roger Hayot's Pan De Maiz
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Low-fat or whole milk, warmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Oil a 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine butter, oil, milk and egg. Whisk until well-blended.
4. Stir flour, baking powder, cornmeal, sugar and salt together with a fork until well-mixed.
5. Add the liquid milk mixture to the dry ingredients and stir just until combined.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish.
7. Bake the cornbread in the preheated oven for about 25 minutes, or until golden on top and a tester inserted in the middle comes out clean.
HINT: If you are in a hurry, use Jiffy cornbread mix (8 1/2 ounce size). To the dry mix, add 1/2 cup evaporated milk, 1 egg and 1 tablespoon melted butter instead of what is called for on the package.
8. Bake at 400°F for 20 minutes. The bread should be golden brown and a tester plunged into its middle should come out clean.
PER SERVING: 330 calories, 5 g protein, 35 g carbohydrate, 19 g fat (6 g saturated), 60 mg cholesterol, 439 mg sodium, 1 g fiber.
